text.Rotherham: So then Joshua came in unto them, suddenly,—all the night, came he up, from Gilgal.

text.NRSV: And the Lord threw them into a panic before Israel, who inflicted a great slaughter on them at Gibeon, chased them by the way of the ascent of Beth-horon, and struck them down as far as Azekah and Makkedah.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh confused them before Israel, and smote them with a great slaughter at Gibeon,—and chased them along the way that goeth up Beth-horon, and smote them as far as Azekah and as far as Makkedah.

text.NRSV: As they fled before Israel, while they were going down the slope of Beth-horon, the Lord threw down huge stones from heaven on them as far as Azekah, and they died; there were more who died because of the hailstones than the Israelites killed with the sword.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when they fled from before Israel, they, being on the slope of Beth-horon, that, Yahweh, cast down upon them great stones out of the heavens, as far as Azekah, and they died,—more, were they who died by the hailstones, than they whom the sons of Israel slew with the sword.

text.NRSV: On the day when the Lord gave the Amorites over to the Israelites, Joshua spoke to the Lord; and he said in the sight of Israel, “Sun, stand still at Gibeon, and Moon, in the valley of Aijalon.”
text.Rotherham: Then, spake Joshua unto Yahweh, on the day when Yahweh delivered up the Amorites before the sons of Israel,—yea he said, in the presence of Israel—Thou Sun! in Gibeon, be still, and thou Moon! in the vale of Aijalon.

text.NRSV: And the sun stood still, and the moon stopped, until the nation took vengeance on their enemies. Is this not written in the Book of Jashar? The sun stopped in mid-heaven, and did not hurry to set for about a whole day.
text.Rotherham: So the sun, was still, and, the moon, stayed, until a nation should be avenged on its foes. Is not, that, written in the Book of the Upright? So then the sun stayed in the middle of the heavens, and hastened not to go in, about a whole day.

text.NRSV: There has been no day like it before or since, when the Lord heeded a human voice; for the Lord fought for Israel.
text.Rotherham: And there was no day like that—before it or after it, when Yahweh hearkened unto the voice of a man,—in that, Yahweh, himself fought for Israel.

text.NRSV: Then Joshua returned, and all Israel with him, to the camp at Gilgal.
text.Rotherham: So Joshua returned, and all Israel with him, unto the camp at Gilgal.

text.NRSV: Meanwhile, these five kings fled and hid themselves in the cave at Makkedah.
text.Rotherham: So these five kings fled,—and hid themselves in a cave, at Makkedah.

text.NRSV: And it was told Joshua, “The five kings have been found, hidden in the cave at Makkedah.”
text.Rotherham: And it was told Joshua, saying,—The five kings are found, hid in a cave at Makkedah.

text.NRSV: Joshua said, “Roll large stones against the mouth of the cave, and set men by it to guard them;
text.Rotherham: And Joshua said, Roll ye great stones against the mouth of the cave,—and set over it men, to watch them;

text.NRSV: but do not stay there yourselves; pursue your enemies, and attack them from the rear. Do not let them enter their towns, for the Lord your God has given them into your hand.”
text.Rotherham: but do not, ye, stay, chase after your enemies, so shall ye attack them in the rear,—do not suffer them to enter into their cities, for Yahweh your God hath delivered them into your hand.

text.NRSV: When Joshua and the Israelites had finished inflicting a very great slaughter on them, until they were wiped out, and when the survivors had entered into the fortified towns,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Joshua and the sons of Israel had made an end of smiting them with a very great slaughter, until they were spent, then did, the remainder that were left of them, enter into the fortified cities.

text.NRSV: all the people returned safe to Joshua in the camp at Makkedah; no one dared to speak against any of the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: And all the people returned unto the camp, unto Joshua at Makkedah, in peace,—none sharpened his tongue at any man of the sons of Israel.

text.NRSV: Then Joshua said, “Open the mouth of the cave, and bring those five kings out to me from the cave.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Joshua, Open ye the mouth of the cave,—and bring out unto me, these five kings, out of the cave.

text.NRSV: They did so, and brought the five kings out to him from the cave, the king of Jerusalem, the king of Hebron, the king of Jarmuth, the king of Lachish, and the king of Eglon.
text.Rotherham: And they did so, and brought out unto him, these five kings, out of the cave,—namely, the king of Jerusalem, the king of Hebron, the king of Jarmuth, the king of Lachish, the king of Eglon.

text.NRSV: When they brought the kings out to Joshua, Joshua summoned all the Israelites, and said to the chiefs of the warriors who had gone with him, “Come near, put your feet on the necks of these kings.” Then they came near and put their feet on their necks.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when they brought out these kings unto Joshua, that Joshua called for all the men of Israel, and said unto the captains of the men of war who had been with him, Come near, put your feet upon the necks of these kings. So they came near, and put their feet upon the necks of them.

text.NRSV: And Joshua said to them, “Do not be afraid or dismayed; be strong and courageous; for thus the Lord will do to all the enemies against whom you fight.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Joshua unto them: Do not fear, nor be alarmed,—be firm and bold, for, thus and thus, will Yahweh do unto all your enemies, against whom ye do fight.

text.NRSV: Afterward Joshua struck them down and put them to death, and he hung them on five trees. And they hung on the trees until evening.
text.Rotherham: And Joshua smote them after this, and put them to death, and hanged them upon five trees,—and they remained hung on the trees, until the evening,

text.NRSV: At sunset Joshua commanded, and they took them down from the trees and threw them into the cave where they had hidden themselves; they set large stones against the mouth of the cave, which remain to this very day.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, at the time of the going in of the sun, that Joshua gave command, and they took them down off the trees, and cast them into the cave, where they had hid themselves,—and put great stones upon the mouth of the cave, [which remain], until this very day.

text.NRSV: Joshua took Makkedah on that day, and struck it and its king with the edge of the sword; he utterly destroyed every person in it; he left no one remaining. And he did to the king of Makkedah as he had done to the king of Jericho.
text.Rotherham: And Joshua captured, Makkedah, on that day, and smote it with the edge of the sword, and, the king thereof, devoted he to destruction, them and all the souls that were therein, he left no survivor,—but did unto the king of Makkedah, as he had done unto the king of Jericho.

text.NRSV: Then Joshua passed on from Makkedah, and all Israel with him, to Libnah, and fought against Libnah.
text.Rotherham: And Joshua passed over, and all Israel with him, from Makkedah unto Libnah,—and fought against Libnah;

text.NRSV: The Lord gave it also and its king into the hand of Israel; and he struck it with the edge of the sword, and every person in it; he left no one remaining in it; and he did to its king as he had done to the king of Jericho.
text.Rotherham: and Yahweh delivered, it also, into the hand of Israel, with the king thereof, and he smote it with the edge of the sword, with all the souls that were therein, he left therein no survivor,—but did unto the king thereof, as he had done unto the king of Jericho.

text.NRSV: Next Joshua passed on from Libnah, and all Israel with him, to Lachish, and laid siege to it, and assaulted it.
text.Rotherham: And Joshua passed over, and all Israel with him, from Libnah unto Lachish,—and encamped against it, and fought against it;

text.NRSV: The Lord gave Lachish into the hand of Israel, and he took it on the second day, and struck it with the edge of the sword, and every person in it, as he had done to Libnah.
text.Rotherham: and Yahweh delivered Lachish into the hand of Israel, and he captured it on the second day, and smote it with the edge of the sword, with all the souls that were therein,—according to all that he had done unto Libnah.

text.NRSV: Then King Horam of Gezer came up to help Lachish; and Joshua struck him and his people, leaving him no survivors.
text.Rotherham: Then, came up Horam, king of Gezer, to help Lachish,—and Joshua smote him and his people, until he had not left him a survivor.

text.NRSV: From Lachish Joshua passed on with all Israel to Eglon; and they laid siege to it, and assaulted it;
text.Rotherham: And Joshua passed over, and all Israel with him, from Lachish unto Eglon,—and they encamped against it, and fought against it;

text.NRSV: and they took it that day, and struck it with the edge of the sword; and every person in it he utterly destroyed that day, as he had done to Lachish.
text.Rotherham: and captured it on the same day, and smote it with the edge of the sword, and, all the souls that were therein, on that day, devoted he to destruction,—according to all that he had done to Lachish.

text.NRSV: Then Joshua went up with all Israel from Eglon to Hebron; they assaulted it,
text.Rotherham: And Joshua went up, and all Israel with him, from Eglon unto Hebron,—and they fought against it;

text.NRSV: and took it, and struck it with the edge of the sword, and its king and its towns, and every person in it; he left no one remaining, just as he had done to Eglon, and utterly destroyed it with every person in it.
text.Rotherham: and captured it, and smote it with the edge of the sword, and the king thereof, and all the cities thereof, with all the souls that were therein—he left not a survivor, according to all that he had done to Eglon,—he devoted it to destruction, and all the souls that were therein.

text.NRSV: Then Joshua, with all Israel, turned back to Debir and assaulted it,
text.Rotherham: Then Joshua turned back, and all Israel with him, unto Debir,—and fought against it;

text.NRSV: and he took it with its king and all its towns; they struck them with the edge of the sword, and utterly destroyed every person in it; he left no one remaining; just as he had done to Hebron, and, as he had done to Libnah and its king, so he did to Debir and its king.
text.Rotherham: and captured it, and the king thereof, and all the cities thereof, and smote them with the edge of the sword, and devoted to destruction every soul that was therein, he left not a survivor,—as he had done unto Hebron, so, did he unto Debir, and unto the king thereof, as also he had done unto Libnah, and unto her king.

text.NRSV: So Joshua defeated the whole land, the hill country and the Negeb and the lowland and the slopes, and all their kings; he left no one remaining, but utterly destroyed all that breathed, as the Lord God of Israel commanded.
text.Rotherham: So Joshua smote all the land—the hill country, and the south, and the lowland, and the slopes, and all their kings, he left not a survivor,—but, every breathing thing, devoted he to destruction, as Yahweh, God of Israel, had commanded.

text.NRSV: And Joshua defeated them from Kadesh-barnea to Gaza, and all the country of Goshen, as far as Gibeon.
text.Rotherham: Yea Joshua smote them, from Kadesh-barnea, even as far as Gaza,—and all the land of Goshen, even as far as Gibeon.

text.NRSV: Joshua took all these kings and their land at one time, because the Lord God of Israel fought for Israel.
text.Rotherham: Yea, all these kings and their land, did Joshua capture at one time,—because, Yahweh, God of Israel, fought for Israel.

text.NRSV: Then Joshua returned, and all Israel with him, to the camp at Gilgal.
text.Rotherham: And Joshua returned, and all Israel with him, unto the camp at Gilgal.

text.NRSV: When King Jabin of Hazor heard of this, he sent to King Jobab of Madon, to the king of Shimron, to the king of Achshaph,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Jabin king of Hazor heard, that he sent unto Jobab king of Madon, and unto the king of Shimron, and unto the king of Achshaph;

text.NRSV: and to the kings who were in the northern hill country, and in the Arabah south of Chinneroth, and in the lowland, and in Naphoth-dor on the west,
text.Rotherham: and unto the kings who were on the north in the hill country and in the waste plain south of Chinneroth, and in the lowland,—and in the heights of Dor, on the west:

text.NRSV: to the Canaanites in the east and the west, the Amorites, the Hittites, the Perizzites, and the Jebusites in the hill country, and the Hivites under Hermon in the land of Mizpah.
text.Rotherham: the Canaanite on the east and on the west, and the Amorite, and the Hittite, and the Perizzite and the Jebusite, in the hill country,—and the Hivite, under Hermon, in the land of Mizpah;

text.NRSV: They came out, with all their troops, a great army, in number like the sand on the seashore, with very many horses and chariots.
text.Rotherham: and they came out—they, and all their hosts with them, much people, like the sand that is upon the seashore for multitude,—with horses and chariots very many.

text.NRSV: All these kings joined their forces, and came and camped together at the waters of Merom, to fight with Israel.
text.Rotherham: And, when all these kings were gathered together, they came in, and encamped as one man, at the waters of Merom, to fight with Israel.

text.NRSV: And the Lord said to Joshua, “Do not be afraid of them, for tomorrow at this time I will hand over all of them, slain, to Israel; you shall hamstring their horses, and burn their chariots with fire.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Joshua—Do not fear because of them, for, to-morrow, about this time, am I going to deliver up all of them, slain, before Israel,—their horses, shalt thou ham-string, and, their chariots, shalt thou burn up with fire.

text.NRSV: So Joshua came suddenly upon them with all his fighting force, by the waters of Merom, and fell upon them.
text.Rotherham: So Joshua, and all the army with him, came in upon them, by the waters of Merom, suddenly, and fell upon them.

text.NRSV: And the Lord handed them over to Israel, who attacked them and chased them as far as Great Sidon and Misrephoth-maim, and eastward as far as the valley of Mizpeh. They struck them down, until they had left no one remaining.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh delivered them up into the hand of Israel, and they smote them, and chased them as far as great Zidon, and as far as Misrephoth-maim, and as far as the valley of Mizpeh, eastward,—yea they smote them until they left them not a survivor.

text.NRSV: And Joshua did to them as the Lord commanded him; he hamstrung their horses, and burned their chariots with fire.
text.Rotherham: And Joshua did unto them, as Yahweh had said unto him,—their horses, he ham-strung, and, their chariots, burned he up with fire.

text.NRSV: Joshua turned back at that time, and took Hazor, and struck its king down with the sword. Before that time Hazor was the head of all those kingdoms.
text.Rotherham: Then Joshua turned back, at that time, and captured Hazor, and, the king thereof, smote he with the sword,—for, Hazor, aforetime, was the head of all these kingdoms.

text.NRSV: And they put to the sword all who were in it, utterly destroying them; there was no one left who breathed, and he burned Hazor with fire.
text.Rotherham: And they smote all the souls that were therein, with the edge of the sword, devoting them to destruction, there was left no breathing thing,—and, Hazor, burned he up with fire.

text.NRSV: And all the towns of those kings, and all their kings, Joshua took, and struck them with the edge of the sword, utterly destroying them, as Moses the servant of the Lord had commanded.
text.Rotherham: And, all the cities of these kings, and all the kings thereof, did Joshua capture, and he smote them with the edge of the sword, devoting them to destruction,—as Moses, the servant of Yahweh, commanded.

text.NRSV: But Israel burned none of the towns that stood on mounds except Hazor, which Joshua did burn.
text.Rotherham: Yet, none of the cities that were still standing upon their mound, did Israel burn, none but Hazor alone, did Joshua burn.

text.NRSV: All the spoil of these towns, and the livestock, the Israelites took for their booty; but all the people they struck down with the edge of the sword, until they had destroyed them, and they did not leave any who breathed.
text.Rotherham: But, all the spoil of these cities, and the cattle, did the sons of Israel take as their prey,—nevertheless, all the human beings, smote they with the edge of the sword, until they had destroyed them, they left remaining no breathing thing.

text.NRSV: As the Lord had commanded his servant Moses, so Moses commanded Joshua, and so Joshua did; he left nothing undone of all that the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: As Yahweh commanded Moses his servant, so, Moses commanded Joshua, and, so, Joshua, did, he set aside nothing of all that Yahweh commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: So Joshua took all that land: the hill country and all the Negeb and all the land of Goshen and the lowland and the Arabah and the hill country of Israel and its lowland,
text.Rotherham: So Joshua took all this land—the hill country, and all the south, and all the land of Goshen, and the lowland, and the waste plain,—and the hill country of Israel, and the lowland thereof:

text.NRSV: from Mount Halak, which rises toward Seir, as far as Baal-gad in the valley of Lebanon below Mount Hermon. He took all their kings, struck them down, and put them to death.
text.Rotherham: from Mount Halak, that goeth up to Seir, even as far as Baal-gad, in the valley of the Lebanon, under Mount Hermon,—and, all their kings, he captured, and smote them, and put them to death.

text.NRSV: Joshua made war a long time with all those kings.
text.Rotherham: Many days, did Joshua, with all these kings, make war.

text.NRSV: There was not a town that made peace with the Israelites, except the Hivites, the inhabitants of Gibeon; all were taken in battle.
text.Rotherham: There was not a city that made peace with the sons of Israel, save the Hivites dwelling in Gibeon,—the whole, took they in battle.

text.NRSV: For it was the Lord’s doing to harden their hearts so that they would come against Israel in battle, in order that they might be utterly destroyed, and might receive no mercy, but be exterminated, just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: For, from Yahweh, came it to pass, that their heart was emboldened to come out to war with Israel, that he might devote them to destruction, that they might find no favour,—but that he might destroy them,—as Yahweh commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: At that time Joshua came and wiped out the Anakim from the hill country, from Hebron, from Debir, from Anab, and from all the hill country of Judah, and from all the hill country of Israel; Joshua utterly destroyed them with their towns.
text.Rotherham: And Joshua came in, at that time, and cut off the Anakim from the hill country—from Hebron, from Debir, from Anab, and from all the hill country of Judah, and from all the hill country of Israel,—with their cities, did Joshua devote them to destruction.

text.NRSV: None of the Anakim was left in the land of the Israelites; some remained only in Gaza, in Gath, and in Ashdod.
text.Rotherham: There was left remaining none of the Anakim, in the land of the sons of Israel,—save only, in Gaza, in Gath, and in Ashdod, did some remain.

text.NRSV: So Joshua took the whole land, according to all that the Lord had spoken to Moses; and Joshua gave it for an inheritance to Israel according to their tribal allotments. And the land had rest from war.
text.Rotherham: So Joshua took the whole land, according to all that Yahweh had spoken unto Moses, and Joshua gave it for an inheritance unto Israel, according to their portions by their tribes,—and, the land, had rest from war.

text.NRSV: Now these are the kings of the land, whom the Israelites defeated, whose land they occupied beyond the Jordan toward the east, from the Wadi Arnon to Mount Hermon, with all the Arabah eastward:
text.Rotherham: Now, these, are the kings of the land, whom the sons of Israel had smitten, and of whose land they had taken possession, over the Jordan, towards the rising of the sun,—from the ravine of Arnon, as far as Mount Hermon, and all the waste plain, on the east:

text.NRSV: King Sihon of the Amorites who lived at Heshbon, and ruled from Aroer, which is on the edge of the Wadi Arnon, and from the middle of the valley as far as the river Jabbok, the boundary of the Ammonites, that is, half of Gilead,
text.Rotherham: Sihon, king of the Amorites, who dwelt in Heshbon,—ruling from Aroer, which is on the edge of the ravine of Arnon, and the middle of the ravine, and half Gilead, even as far as the ravine Jabbok, the boundary of the sons of Ammon;

text.NRSV: and the Arabah to the Sea of Chinneroth eastward, and in the direction of Beth-jeshimoth, to the sea of the Arabah, the Dead Sea, southward to the foot of the slopes of Pisgah;
text.Rotherham: and the waste plain as far as the sea of Chinneroth, on the east, even as far as the sea of the waste plain, the salt sea, on the east, the way to Bethjeshimoth,—and, on the south, under the slopes of Pisgah;

text.NRSV: and King Og of Bashan, one of the last of the Rephaim, who lived at Ashtaroth and at Edrei
text.Rotherham: and the boundary of Og, king of Bashan, of the remnant of the giants,—him who dwelt in Ashtaroth and in Edrei;

text.NRSV: and ruled over Mount Hermon and Salecah and all Bashan to the boundary of the Geshurites and the Maacathites, and over half of Gilead to the boundary of King Sihon of Heshbon.
text.Rotherham: ruling also in Mount Hermon, and in Salecah, and in all Bashan, as far as the boundary of the Geshurites, and the Maacathites,—and half Gilead, the boundary of Sihon king of Heshbon:—

text.NRSV: Moses, the servant of the Lord , and the Israelites defeated them; and Moses the servant of the Lord gave their land for a possession to the Reubenites and the Gadites and the half-tribe of Manasseh.
text.Rotherham: Moses the servant of Yahweh, and the sons of Israel, had smitten them,—and Moses the servant of Yahweh, had given it, as a possession, unto the Reubenites, and unto the Gadites, and unto the half tribe of Manasseh.

text.NRSV: The following are the kings of the land whom Joshua and the Israelites defeated on the west side of the Jordan, from Baal-gad in the valley of Lebanon to Mount Halak, that rises toward Seir (and Joshua gave their land to the tribes of Israel as a possession according to their allotments,
text.Rotherham: And, these, are the kings of the land, whom Joshua and the sons of Israel smote over the Jordan, towards the west, from Baal-gad in the valley of the Lebanon, even as far as the Mount Halak that goeth up towards Seir,—and Joshua gave it unto the tribes of Israel, as a possession, according to their portions;

text.NRSV: in the hill country, in the lowland, in the Arabah, in the slopes, in the wilderness, and in the Negeb, the land of the Hittites, Amorites, Canaanites, Perizzites, Hivites, and Jebusites):
text.Rotherham: in the hill country and in the lowland, and in the waste plain, and in the slopes, and in the desert, and in the south,—the Hittite, the Amorite, and the Canaanite, the Perizzite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite:—

text.NRSV: the king of Jericho one the king of Ai, which is next to Bethel one
text.Rotherham: The king of Jericho, one, the king of Ai, which is beside Bethel, one,

text.NRSV: the king of Jerusalem one the king of Hebron one
text.Rotherham: The king of Jerusalem, one, the king of Hebron, one,

text.NRSV: the king of Jarmuth one the king of Lachish one
text.Rotherham: The king of Jarmuth, one, the king of Lachish, one,

text.NRSV: the king of Eglon one the king of Gezer one
text.Rotherham: The king of Eglon, one, the king of Gezer, one,

text.NRSV: the king of Debir one the king of Geder one
text.Rotherham: The king of Debir, one, the king of Geder, one,

text.NRSV: the king of Hormah one the king of Arad one
text.Rotherham: The king of Hormah, one, the king of Arad, one,

text.NRSV: the king of Libnah one the king of Adullam one
text.Rotherham: The king of Libnah, one, the king of Adullam, one,

text.NRSV: the king of Makkedah one the king of Bethel one
text.Rotherham: The king of Makkedah, one, the king of Bethel, one,

text.NRSV: the king of Tappuah one the king of Hepher one
text.Rotherham: The king of Tappuah, one, the king of Hepher, one,

text.NRSV: the king of Aphek one the king of Lasharon one
text.Rotherham: The king of Aphek, one, the king of Lassharon, one,

text.NRSV: the king of Madon one the king of Hazor one
text.Rotherham: The king of Madon, one, the king of Hazor, one,

text.NRSV: the king of Shimron-meron one the king of Achshaph one
text.Rotherham: The king of Shimron-meron, one, the king of Achshaph, one,

text.NRSV: the king of Taanach one the king of Megiddo one
text.Rotherham: The king of Taanach, one, the king of Megiddo, one,

text.NRSV: the king of Kedesh one the king of Jokneam in Carmel one
text.Rotherham: The king of Kadesh, one, the king of Jokneam in Carmel, one,

text.NRSV: the king of Dor in Naphath-dor one the king of Goiim in Galilee, one
text.Rotherham: The king of Dor, in the height of Dor, one, the king of Goim in Gilgal, one,

text.NRSV: the king of Tirzah one thirty-one kings in all.
text.Rotherham: The king of Tirzah, one,—All the kings, thirty and one.

text.NRSV: Now Joshua was old and advanced in years; and the Lord said to him, “You are old and advanced in years, and very much of the land still remains to be possessed.
text.Rotherham: Now, Joshua, was old, advanced in days,—so then Yahweh said unto him—Thou, art old, advanced in days, and, much the larger part of the land, remaineth to be possessed.

text.NRSV: This is the land that still remains: all the regions of the Philistines, and all those of the Geshurites
text.Rotherham: This, is the land that remaineth,—all the regions of the Philistines, and all the Geshuri;

text.NRSV: (from the Shihor, which is east of Egypt, northward to the boundary of Ekron, it is reckoned as Canaanite; there are five rulers of the Philistines, those of Gaza, Ashdod, Ashkelon, Gath, and Ekron), and those of the Avvim,
text.Rotherham: from the Shihor which faceth Egypt, even as far as the boundary of Ekron, northward, to the Canaanites, is it counted,—five princes of Philistines, the Gazites, and the Ashdodites, the Ashkalonites, the Gittites, and the Ekronites, also the Avvim

text.NRSV: in the south, all the land of the Canaanites, and Mearah that belongs to the Sidonians, to Aphek, to the boundary of the Amorites,
text.Rotherham: on the south: all the land of the Canaanites, and Mearah which belongeth to the Zidonians, as far as Aphek,—as far as the boundary of the Amorites;

text.NRSV: and the land of the Gebalites, and all Lebanon, toward the east, from Baal-gad below Mount Hermon to Lebo-hamath,
text.Rotherham: and the land of the Gebalites, and all the Lebanon, to the rising of the sun, from Baal-gad, under Mount Hermon,—as far as the entering in of Hamath:

text.NRSV: all the inhabitants of the hill country from Lebanon to Misrephoth-maim, even all the Sidonians. I will myself drive them out from before the Israelites; only allot the land to Israel for an inheritance, as I have commanded you.
text.Rotherham: all the inhabitants of the hill country, from the Lebanon as far as Misrephoth-maim—all the Zidonians, I myself, will dispossess them from before the sons of Israel,—nevertheless, assign thou it by lot unto Israel, for an inheritance, as I have commanded thee.

text.NRSV: Now therefore divide this land for an inheritance to the nine tribes and the half-tribe of Manasseh.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, apportion this land as an inheritance, unto the nine tribes,—and the half tribe of Manasseh.

text.NRSV: With the other half-tribe of Manasseh the Reubenites and the Gadites received their inheritance, which Moses gave them, beyond the Jordan eastward, as Moses the servant of the Lord gave them:
text.Rotherham: With them, the, Reubenites and the Gadites, received their inheritance,—which Moses gave them beyond the Jordan, eastward, as Moses the servant of Yahweh gave unto them:

text.NRSV: from Aroer, which is on the edge of the Wadi Arnon, and the town that is in the middle of the valley, and all the tableland from Medeba as far as Dibon;
text.Rotherham: from Aroer, which is on the edge of the ravine of Arnon, and the city, which is in the midst of the ravine, and all the table-land of Medeba, as far as Dibon;

text.NRSV: and all the cities of King Sihon of the Amorites, who reigned in Heshbon, as far as the boundary of the Ammonites;
text.Rotherham: and all the cities of Sihon, king of the Amorites, who reigned in Heshbon,—as far as the boundary of the sons of Ammon;

text.NRSV: and Gilead, and the region of the Geshurites and Maacathites, and all Mount Hermon, and all Bashan to Salecah;
text.Rotherham: and Gilead, and the boundary of the Geshurites and the Maacathites, and all Mount Hermon, and all Bashan, as far as Salecah;

text.NRSV: all the kingdom of Og in Bashan, who reigned in Ashtaroth and in Edrei (he alone was left of the survivors of the Rephaim); these Moses had defeated and driven out.
text.Rotherham: all the kingdom of Og, in Bashan, who reigned in Ashtaroth and in Edrei,—he, being left of the remnant of the giants; and Moses smote them and dispossessed them;

text.NRSV: Yet the Israelites did not drive out the Geshurites or the Maacathites; but Geshur and Maacath live within Israel to this day.
text.Rotherham: the sons of Israel, however, did not dispossess, the Geshurites or the Maacathites,—but Geshur and Maacath have remained in the midst of Israel until this day.

text.NRSV: To the tribe of Levi alone Moses gave no inheritance; the offerings by fire to the Lord God of Israel are their inheritance, as he said to them.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, unto the tribe of Levi, gave he no inheritance,—the altar-flames of Yahweh God of Israel, are his inheritance, as he spake unto him.

text.NRSV: Moses gave an inheritance to the tribe of the Reubenites according to their clans.
text.Rotherham: Thus then Moses gave [an inheritance] unto the tribe of the sons of Reuben by their families;

text.NRSV: Their territory was from Aroer, which is on the edge of the Wadi Arnon, and the town that is in the middle of the valley, and all the tableland by Medeba;
text.Rotherham: and their boundary was from Aroer, that is on the edge of the ravine of Arnon and the city which is in the midst of the ravine and all the table-land by Medeba;

text.NRSV: with Heshbon, and all its towns that are in the tableland; Dibon, and Bamoth-baal, and Beth-baal-meon,
text.Rotherham: Heshbon and all her cities which are on the table-land,—Dibon and Bamoth-baal, and Beth-baal-meon;

text.NRSV: and Jahaz, and Kedemoth, and Mephaath,
text.Rotherham: and Jahaz and Kedemoth and Maphaath;

text.NRSV: and Kiriathaim, and Sibmah, and Zereth-shahar on the hill of the valley,
text.Rotherham: and Kiriathaim and Sibmah, and Zareth-shahar on the mount of the vale;

text.NRSV: and Beth-peor, and the slopes of Pisgah, and Beth-jeshimoth,
text.Rotherham: and Beth-peor, and the slopes of Pisgah, and Beth-jeshimoth;

text.NRSV: that is, all the towns of the tableland, and all the kingdom of King Sihon of the Amorites, who reigned in Heshbon, whom Moses defeated with the leaders of Midian, Evi and Rekem and Zur and Hur and Reba, as princes of Sihon, who lived in the land.
text.Rotherham: and all the cities of the table-land, and all the kingdom of Sihon king of the Amorites, who reigned in Heshbon,—whom Moses smote—with the princes of Midian, Evi and Rekem and Zur and Hur and Reba, dukes of Sihon dwelling in the land;

text.NRSV: Along with the rest of those they put to death, the Israelites also put to the sword Balaam son of Beor, who practiced divination.
text.Rotherham: Balaam also son of Beor, the diviner, did the sons of Israel slay with the sword among their slain.

text.NRSV: And the border of the Reubenites was the Jordan and its banks. This was the inheritance of the Reubenites, according to their families with their towns and villages.
text.Rotherham: And so the boundary of the sons of Reuben was the Jordan and district. This, is the inheritance of the sons of Reuben by their families, the cities and the villages thereof.

text.NRSV: Moses gave an inheritance also to the tribe of the Gadites, according to their families.
text.Rotherham: And Moses gave [an inheritance] unto the tribe of Gad, unto the sons of Gad by their families;

text.NRSV: Their territory was Jazer, and all the towns of Gilead, and half the land of the Ammonites, to Aroer, which is east of Rabbah,
text.Rotherham: and their boundary was—Jazer, and all the cities of Gilead, and half the land of the sons of Ammon,—as far as Aroer, which faceth Rabbah;

text.NRSV: and from Heshbon to Ramath-mizpeh and Betonim, and from Mahanaim to the territory of Debir,
text.Rotherham: and from Heshbon as far as Ramath-mizpeh, and Botonim,—and from Mahanaim as far as the boundary of Debir;

text.NRSV: and in the valley Beth-haram, Beth-nimrah, Succoth, and Zaphon, the rest of the kingdom of King Sihon of Heshbon, the Jordan and its banks, as far as the lower end of the Sea of Chinnereth, eastward beyond the Jordan.
text.Rotherham: and in the valley—Beth-haram and Beth-nimrah and Succoth and Zaphon, the rest of the kingdom of Sihon king of Heshbon, the Jordan and district,—as far as the end of the sea of Chinnereth, beyond the Jordan eastward.

text.NRSV: This is the inheritance of the Gadites according to their clans, with their towns and villages.
text.Rotherham: This, is the inheritance of the sons of Gad, by their families,—the cities and the villages thereof.

text.NRSV: Moses gave an inheritance to the half-tribe of Manasseh; it was allotted to the half-tribe of the Manassites according to their families.
text.Rotherham: And Moses gave [an inheritance] unto the half tribe of Manasseh,—so it belonged to the half tribe of the sons of Manasseh by their families;

text.NRSV: Their territory extended from Mahanaim, through all Bashan, the whole kingdom of King Og of Bashan, and all the settlements of Jair, which are in Bashan, sixty towns,
text.Rotherham: and their boundary was—from Mahanaim all Bashan all the kingdom of Og king of Bashan and all the Encampments of Jair which are in Bashan three-score cities;

text.NRSV: and half of Gilead, and Ashtaroth, and Edrei, the towns of the kingdom of Og in Bashan; these were allotted to the people of Machir son of Manasseh according to their clans—for half the Machirites.
text.Rotherham: and, half Gilead and Ashtaroth and Edrei, cities of the kingdom of Og in Bashan, pertained to the sons of Machir son of Manasseh, to half the sons of Machir by their families.

text.NRSV: These are the inheritances that Moses distributed in the plains of Moab, beyond the Jordan east of Jericho.
text.Rotherham: These, are [the portions] which Moses gave for inheritance, in the waste plains of Moab,—over the Jordan by Jericho eastward.

text.NRSV: But to the tribe of Levi Moses gave no inheritance; the Lord God of Israel is their inheritance, as he said to them.
text.Rotherham: But, unto the tribe of Levi, Moses gave no inheritance,—Yahweh God of Israel, he, was their inheritance, as he spake unto them.

text.NRSV: These are the inheritances that the Israelites received in the land of Canaan, which the priest Eleazar, and Joshua son of Nun, and the heads of the families of the tribes of the Israelites distributed to them.
text.Rotherham: And, these, are the inheritances which the sons of Israel received in the land of Canaan,—which Eleazar the priest, and Joshua the son of Nun, and the ancestral heads of the tribes of the sons of Israel gave them to inherit.

text.NRSV: Their inheritance was by lot, as the Lord had commanded Moses for the nine and one-half tribes.
text.Rotherham: By lot, was their inheritance divided,—as Yahweh commanded by the hand of Moses, unto the nine tribes and unto the half tribe.

text.NRSV: For Moses had given an inheritance to the two and one-half tribes beyond the Jordan; but to the Levites he gave no inheritance among them.
text.Rotherham: For Moses had given the two tribes and the half tribe their inheritance over the Jordan,—but, unto the Levites, gave he no inheritance in their midst;

text.NRSV: For the people of Joseph were two tribes, Manasseh and Ephraim; and no portion was given to the Levites in the land, but only towns to live in, with their pasture lands for their flocks and herds.
text.Rotherham: for the sons of Joseph became two tribes: Manasseh and Ephraim,—so they gave no portion to the Levites in the land, save only cities to dwell in, with the pasture lands thereof, for their cattle and for their substance.

text.NRSV: The Israelites did as the Lord commanded Moses; they allotted the land.
text.Rotherham: As Yahweh commanded Moses, so, did the sons of Israel,—when they apportioned the land.

text.NRSV: Then the people of Judah came to Joshua at Gilgal; and Caleb son of Jephunneh the Kenizzite said to him, “You know what the Lord said to Moses the man of God in Kadesh-barnea concerning you and me.
text.Rotherham: Then came near the sons of Judah unto Joshua in Gilgal, and Caleb the son of Jephunneh, the Kenizzite, said unto him,—Thou, knowest the word which Yahweh spake unto Moses the man of God in my behalf and in thine in Kadesh-barnea.

text.NRSV: I was forty years old when Moses the servant of the Lord sent me from Kadesh-barnea to spy out the land; and I brought him an honest report.
text.Rotherham: Forty years old, was I when Moses the servant of Yahweh sent me from Kadesh-barnea to spy out the land,—so I brought him back word, as was in my heart.

text.NRSV: But my companions who went up with me made the heart of the people melt; yet I wholeheartedly followed the Lord my God.
text.Rotherham: But, my brethren who had been up with me, made the heart of the people melt,—whereas, I, wholly followed Yahweh my God.

text.NRSV: And Moses swore on that day, saying, ‘Surely the land on which your foot has trodden shall be an inheritance for you and your children forever, because you have wholeheartedly followed the Lord my God.’
text.Rotherham: So then Moses sware on that day saying, Surely, the land on which thy foot hath trodden, unto thee, shall belong, for an inheritance, and unto thy sons, unto times age-abiding,—because thou hast wholly followed Yahweh my God.

text.NRSV: And now, as you see, the Lord has kept me alive, as he said, these forty-five years since the time that the Lord spoke this word to Moses, while Israel was journeying through the wilderness; and here I am today, eighty-five years old.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore lo! Yahweh hath kept me alive as he spake these forty and five years from the time Yahweh spake this word unto Moses, while Israel journeyed in the desert. Now, therefore lo! I—to-day, am eighty and five years old:

text.NRSV: I am still as strong today as I was on the day that Moses sent me; my strength now is as my strength was then, for war, and for going and coming.
text.Rotherham: I remain, to-day, as courageous as on the day when Moses sent me, as my strength was then, so also my strength is now,—for war, both to go out and to come in.

text.NRSV: So now give me this hill country of which the Lord spoke on that day; for you heard on that day how the Anakim were there, with great fortified cities; it may be that the Lord will be with me, and I shall drive them out, as the Lord said.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, give me this mountain, whereof Yahweh spake, on that day,—for, thou thyself, didst hear, on that day, that, Anakim, were there, and great cities fortified, if so be Yahweh be with me, then shall I dispossess them, as spake Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Then Joshua blessed him, and gave Hebron to Caleb son of Jephunneh for an inheritance.
text.Rotherham: So Joshua blessed him, and gave Hebron unto Caleb son of Jephunneh, for an inheritance.

text.NRSV: So Hebron became the inheritance of Caleb son of Jephunneh the Kenizzite to this day, because he wholeheartedly followed the Lord, the God of Israel.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, hath Hebron belonged unto Caleb son of Jephunneh the Kenezzite, for an inheritance, unto this day,—because he wholly followed Yahweh, God of Israel.

text.NRSV: Now the name of Hebron formerly was Kiriath-arba; this Arba was the greatest man among the Anakim. And the land had rest from war.
text.Rotherham: Now, the name of Hebron, was formerly, City of Arba, the greatest man among the Anakim, was he. And, the land, had rest from war.

text.NRSV: The lot for the tribe of the people of Judah according to their families reached southward to the boundary of Edom, to the wilderness of Zin at the farthest south.
text.Rotherham: So then the boundary of the tribe of the sons of Judah, by their families,—reached unto the boundary of Edom, the desert of Zin southward, on the extreme south;

text.NRSV: And their south boundary ran from the end of the Dead Sea, from the bay that faces southward;
text.Rotherham: so their south boundary beginneth at the end of the Salt Sea,—from the bay that turneth to the south;

text.NRSV: it goes out southward of the ascent of Akrabbim, passes along to Zin, and goes up south of Kadesh-barnea, along by Hezron, up to Addar, makes a turn to Karka,
text.Rotherham: and goeth forth on the south of the cliffs of Akrabbim, and crosseth over to Zin, and goeth up on the south of Kadesh-barnea,—and passeth over by Hezron, and goeth up to Addar, and turneth round to Karka;

text.NRSV: passes along to Azmon, goes out by the Wadi of Egypt, and comes to its end at the sea. This shall be your south boundary.
text.Rotherham: and passeth over to Azmon, and goeth forth at the ravine of Egypt, and so the extensions of the boundary are to the sea. This, shall be your south boundary.

text.NRSV: And the east boundary is the Dead Sea, to the mouth of the Jordan. And the boundary on the north side runs from the bay of the sea at the mouth of the Jordan;
text.Rotherham: And, a boundary eastward, is the Salt Sea, as far as the end of the Jordan. And, the boundary on the north side, is from the bay of the sea, from the end of the Jordan.

text.NRSV: and the boundary goes up to Beth-hoglah, and passes along north of Beth-arabah; and the boundary goes up to the Stone of Bohan, Reuben’s son;
text.Rotherham: And the boundary goeth up Beth-hoglah, and passeth over, on the north, by Beth-arabah,—and the boundary goeth up by the Stone of Bohan, son of Reuben;

text.NRSV: and the boundary goes up to Debir from the Valley of Achor, and so northward, turning toward Gilgal, which is opposite the ascent of Adummim, which is on the south side of the valley; and the boundary passes along to the waters of En-shemesh, and ends at En-rogel;
text.Rotherham: and the boundary goeth up towards Debir, out of the vale of Achor, then northward, turning unto Gilgal, which is over against the ascent of Adummim, which is on the south side of the ravine,—then the boundary passeth over unto the waters of En-shemesh, and so the extensions thereof are unto En-rogel:

text.NRSV: then the boundary goes up by the valley of the son of Hinnom at the southern slope of the Jebusites (that is, Jerusalem); and the boundary goes up to the top of the mountain that lies over against the valley of Hinnom, on the west, at the northern end of the valley of Rephaim;
text.Rotherham: then ascendeth the boundary by the valley of the son of Hinnom, to the side of the Jebusite, on the south, the same, is Jerusalem,—and the boundary goeth up unto the top of the mountain which faceth the valley of Hinnom, to the west, which is at the end of the Vale of Giants, northwards;

text.NRSV: then the boundary extends from the top of the mountain to the spring of the Waters of Nephtoah, and from there to the towns of Mount Ephron; then the boundary bends around to Baalah (that is, Kiriath-jearim);
text.Rotherham: and the boundary turneth round, from the top of the mountain, unto the fountain of the waters of Nephtoah, and goeth out unto the cities of Mount Ephron,—then the boundary turneth round to Baalah, the same, is Kiriath-jearim;

text.NRSV: and the boundary circles west of Baalah to Mount Seir, passes along to the northern slope of Mount Jearim (that is, Chesalon), and goes down to Beth-shemesh, and passes along by Timnah;
text.Rotherham: then the boundary goeth round from Baalah westward, unto Mount Seir, and passeth over unto the slope of Mount Yearim, on the north, the same, is Chesalon,—and descendeth Bethshemesh, and passeth over Timnah;

text.NRSV: the boundary goes out to the slope of the hill north of Ekron, then the boundary bends around to Shikkeron, and passes along to Mount Baalah, and goes out to Jabneel; then the boundary comes to an end at the sea.
text.Rotherham: then the boundary goeth forth unto the side of Ekron, northward, then the boundary turneth round towards Shikkeron, and crosseth over Mount Baalah, and goeth out at Jabneel,—and the extensions of the boundary are to the sea.

text.NRSV: And the west boundary was the Mediterranean with its coast. This is the boundary surrounding the people of Judah according to their families.
text.Rotherham: And, as a west boundary, are the great sea and coast. This, is the boundary of the sons of Judah, round about, by their families.

text.NRSV: According to the commandment of the Lord to Joshua, he gave to Caleb son of Jephunneh a portion among the people of Judah, Kiriath-arba, that is, Hebron (Arba was the father of Anak).
text.Rotherham: And, to Caleb, son of Jephunneh, gave he a portion, in the midst of the sons of Judah, at the bidding of Yahweh, by Joshua,—even the City of Arba, father of Anak, the same, is Hebron.

text.NRSV: And Caleb drove out from there the three sons of Anak: Sheshai, Ahiman, and Talmai, the descendants of Anak.
text.Rotherham: And Caleb dispossessed from thence, the three sons of Anak,—Sheshai, and Ahiman, and Talmai, the descendants of Anak.

text.NRSV: From there he went up against the inhabitants of Debir; now the name of Debir formerly was Kiriath-sepher.
text.Rotherham: And he went up from thence, against the inhabitants of Debir,—now, the name of Debir, formerly, was Kiriath-sepher.

text.NRSV: And Caleb said, “Whoever attacks Kiriath-sepher and takes it, to him I will give my daughter Achsah as wife.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Caleb, He that smiteth Kiriath-sepher, and captureth it, I will give unto him Achsah, my daughter, to wife.

text.NRSV: Othniel son of Kenaz, the brother of Caleb, took it; and he gave him his daughter Achsah as wife.
text.Rotherham: And Othniel son of Kenaz, brother of Caleb, captured it,—so he gave unto him Achsah, his daughter, to wife.

text.NRSV: When she came to him, she urged him to ask her father for a field. As she dismounted from her donkey, Caleb said to her, “What do you wish?”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when she came, that she moved him to ask of her father a field, and, when she alighted from off the ass, Caleb said unto her—What aileth thee?

text.NRSV: She said to him, “Give me a present; since you have set me in the land of the Negeb, give me springs of water as well.” So Caleb gave her the upper springs and the lower springs.
text.Rotherham: And she said—Give me a present, for, dry land, hast thou given me, therefore must thou give me, pools of water. So he gave her upper pools and lower pools.

text.NRSV: This is the inheritance of the tribe of the people of Judah according to their families.
text.Rotherham: This, is the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Judah, by their families.

text.NRSV: The towns belonging to the tribe of the people of Judah in the extreme South, toward the boundary of Edom, were Kabzeel, Eder, Jagur,
text.Rotherham: And the uttermost cities of the tribe of the sons of Judah, towards the boundary of Edom in the south, were,—Kabzeel and Eder, and Jagur,

text.NRSV: Kinah, Dimonah, Adadah,
text.Rotherham: and Kinah and Dimonah, and Adadah,—

text.NRSV: Kedesh, Hazor, Ithnan,
text.Rotherham: and Kedesh and Hazor, and Ithnan,

text.NRSV: Ziph, Telem, Bealoth,
text.Rotherham: Ziph and Talem, and Bealoth,

text.NRSV: Hazor-hadattah, Kerioth-hezron (that is, Hazor),
text.Rotherham: and Hazor-hadattah and Kerioth-hezron, the same, is Hazor;

text.NRSV: Amam, Shema, Moladah,
text.Rotherham: Amam and Shema, and Moladah,

text.NRSV: Hazar-gaddah, Heshmon, Beth-pelet,
text.Rotherham: and Hazar-gaddah and Heshmon, and Beth-pelet.

text.NRSV: Hazar-shual, Beer-sheba, Biziothiah,
text.Rotherham: and Hazarshual and Beer-sheba, with the hamlets thereof,

text.NRSV: Baalah, Iim, Ezem,
text.Rotherham: Baalah and Iyyim, and Ezem,

text.NRSV: Eltolad, Chesil, Hormah,
text.Rotherham: and Eltolad and Chesil, and Hormah,

text.NRSV: Ziklag, Madmannah, Sansannah,
text.Rotherham: and Ziklag and Madmannah, and Sansannah,

text.NRSV: Lebaoth, Shilhim, Ain, and Rimmon: in all, twenty-nine towns, with their villages.
text.Rotherham: and Lebaoth and Shilhim, and En-rimmon,—all the cities are twenty-nine, with their villages.

text.NRSV: And in the Lowland, Eshtaol, Zorah, Ashnah,
text.Rotherham: In the lowland,—Eshtaol and Zorah, and Ashnah,

text.NRSV: Zanoah, En-gannim, Tappuah, Enam,
text.Rotherham: and Zanoah and En-gannim, Tappuah, and Enam;

text.NRSV: Jarmuth, Adullam, Socoh, Azekah,
text.Rotherham: Jarmuth, and Adullam, Socoh, and Azekah;

text.NRSV: Shaaraim, Adithaim, Gederah, Gederothaim: fourteen towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: and Shaaraim, and Adithaim, and Gederah, and Gederothaim,—fourteen cities, with their villages.

text.NRSV: Zenan, Hadashah, Migdal-gad,
text.Rotherham: Zenan and Hadashah, and Migdal-gad,

text.NRSV: Dilan, Mizpeh, Jokthe-el,
text.Rotherham: and Dilan and Mizpeh, and Joktheel,

text.NRSV: Lachish, Bozkath, Eglon,
text.Rotherham: Lachish and Bozkath, and Eglon,

text.NRSV: Cabbon, Lahmam, Chitlish,
text.Rotherham: and Cabbon and Lahmas, and Chithlish,

text.NRSV: Gederoth, Beth-dagon, Naamah, and Makkedah: sixteen towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: and Gederoth, Beth-dagon and Naamah, and Makkedah,—sixteen cities, with their villages.

text.NRSV: Libnah, Ether, Ashan,
text.Rotherham: Libnah and Ether, and Ashan,

text.NRSV: Iphtah, Ashnah, Nezib,
text.Rotherham: and Iphtah and Ashnah, and Nezib,

text.NRSV: Keilah, Achzib, and Mareshah: nine towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: and Keilah and Achzib, and Mareshah,—nine cities, with their villages.

text.NRSV: Ekron, with its dependencies and its villages;
text.Rotherham: Ekron with her towns, and her villages.

text.NRSV: from Ekron to the sea, all that were near Ashdod, with their villages.
text.Rotherham: From Ekron, even unto the sea,—all that were by the side of Ashdod with their villages.

text.NRSV: Ashdod, its towns and its villages; Gaza, its towns and its villages; to the Wadi of Egypt, and the Great Sea with its coast.
text.Rotherham: Ashdod, her towns and her villages. Gaza, her towns and her villages, as far as the ravine of Egypt,—and the great sea and coast.

text.NRSV: And in the hill country, Shamir, Jattir, Socoh,
text.Rotherham: And in the hill-country,—Shamir and Jattir, and Socoh,

text.NRSV: Dannah, Kiriath-sannah (that is, Debir),
text.Rotherham: and Dannah and Kiriath-sannah, the same, is Debir,

text.NRSV: Anab, Eshtemoh, Anim,
text.Rotherham: and Anab and Eshtemoh, and Anim,

text.NRSV: Goshen, Holon, and Giloh: eleven towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: and Goshen and Holon, and Giloh,—eleven cities, with their villages.

text.NRSV: Arab, Dumah, Eshan,
text.Rotherham: Arab and Rumah and Eshan,

text.NRSV: Janim, Beth-tappuah, Aphekah,
text.Rotherham: and Janim and Beth-tappuah and Aphekah,

text.NRSV: Humtah, Kiriath-arba (that is, Hebron), and Zior: nine towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: and Humtah, and Kiriath-arba, the same, is Hebron, and Zior,—nine cities, with their villages.

text.NRSV: Maon, Carmel, Ziph, Juttah,
text.Rotherham: Maon, Carmel, and Ziph and Jutah,

text.NRSV: Jezreel, Jokdeam, Zanoah,
text.Rotherham: and Jezreel and Jokdeam, and Zanoah,

text.NRSV: Kain, Gibeah, and Timnah: ten towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: Kain, Gibeah and Timnah,—ten cities, with their villages.

text.NRSV: Halhul, Beth-zur, Gedor,
text.Rotherham: Halhul, Beth-zur, and Gedor,

text.NRSV: Maarath, Beth-anoth, and Eltekon: six towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: and Maarath and Beth-anoth, and Eltekon,—six cities, with their villages.

text.NRSV: Kiriath-baal (that is, Kiriath-jearim), and Rabbah: two towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: Kiriath-baal, the same, is Kiriath-jearim, and Rabbah,—two cities, with their villages.

text.NRSV: In the wilderness, Beth-arabah, Middin, Secacah,
text.Rotherham: In the desert,—Beth-arabah, Middin, and Secacah,

text.NRSV: Nibshan, the City of Salt, and En-gedi: six towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: and Nibshan and the City of Salt, and En-gedi,—six cities, with their villages.

text.NRSV: But the people of Judah could not drive out the Jebusites, the inhabitants of Jerusalem; so the Jebusites live with the people of Judah in Jerusalem to this day.
text.Rotherham: But, as for the Jebusites, the inhabitants of Jerusalem, the sons of Judah could not dispossess them,—so the Jebusites have dwelt with the sons of Judah, in Jerusalem, until this day.

text.NRSV: The allotment of the Josephites went from the Jordan by Jericho, east of the waters of Jericho, into the wilderness, going up from Jericho into the hill country to Bethel;
text.Rotherham: Then came out the lot, for the sons of Joseph, from the Jordan by Jericho, at the waters of Jericho, eastward,—the desert, going up from Jericho, through the hill country to Bethel;

text.NRSV: then going from Bethel to Luz, it passes along to Ataroth, the territory of the Archites;
text.Rotherham: and it goeth out from Bethel towards Luz,—and passeth over unto the boundary of the Archites at Ataroth;

text.NRSV: then it goes down westward to the territory of the Japhletites, as far as the territory of Lower Beth-horon, then to Gezer, and it ends at the sea.
text.Rotherham: and descendeth westward, unto the boundary of the Japhletites, as far as the boundary of Bethhoron the nether, and as far as Gezer,—and the extensions thereof are to the sea.

text.NRSV: The Josephites—Manasseh and Ephraim—received their inheritance.
text.Rotherham: So the sons of Joseph, Manasseh and Ephraim, received their inheritance.

text.NRSV: The territory of the Ephraimites by their families was as follows: the boundary of their inheritance on the east was Ataroth-addar as far as Upper Beth-horon,
text.Rotherham: And the boundary of the sons of Ephraim, by their families, was,—yea the boundary of their inheritance on the east, was Ataroth-addar, as far as Beth-horon the upper;

text.NRSV: and the boundary goes from there to the sea; on the north is Michmethath; then on the east the boundary makes a turn toward Taanath-shiloh, and passes along beyond it on the east to Janoah,
text.Rotherham: and the boundary goeth out westward at Michmethath, on the north, then the boundary bendeth round easward, to Taanath-shiloh,—and passeth by it on the east to Janoah;

text.NRSV: then it goes down from Janoah to Ataroth and to Naarah, and touches Jericho, ending at the Jordan.
text.Rotherham: and goeth down from Janoah to Ataroth and to Naarah,—and toucheth upon Jericho, and goeth out at the Jordan.

text.NRSV: From Tappuah the boundary goes westward to the Wadi Kanah, and ends at the sea. Such is the inheritance of the tribe of the Ephraimites by their families,
text.Rotherham: From Tappuah, runneth the boundary along westward, to the ravine of Kanah, and the extensions thereof are to the sea,—this, is the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Ephraim, by their families;

text.NRSV: together with the towns that were set apart for the Ephraimites within the inheritance of the Manassites, all those towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: together with the cities which were separated for the sons of Ephraim, in the midst of the inheritance of the sons of Manasseh,—all the cities, with their villages.

text.NRSV: They did not, however, drive out the Canaanites who lived in Gezer: so the Canaanites have lived within Ephraim to this day but have been made to do forced labor.
text.Rotherham: But they did not dispossess the Canaanites who were dwelling in Gazer,—so the Canaanites have dwelt in the midst of Ephraim unto this day, and have become tributary servants.

text.NRSV: Then allotment was made to the tribe of Manasseh, for he was the firstborn of Joseph. To Machir the firstborn of Manasseh, the father of Gilead, were allotted Gilead and Bashan, because he was a warrior.
text.Rotherham: And the lot for the tribe of Manasseh was, for, he, was the firstborn of Joseph,—as for Machir, the firstborn of Manasseh, the father of Gilead, because, he, was a man of war, therefore had he Gilead and Bashan.

text.NRSV: And allotments were made to the rest of the tribe of Manasseh, by their families, Abiezer, Helek, Asriel, Shechem, Hepher, and Shemida; these were the male descendants of Manasseh son of Joseph, by their families.
text.Rotherham: So the lot pertained to the rest of the sons of Manasseh, by their families, to the sons of Abiezer, and to the sons of Helek, and to the sons of Asriel, and to the sons of Shechem, and to the sons of Hepher, and to the sons of Shemida,—these, were the sons of Manasseh, son of Joseph, even the male descendants, by their families.

text.NRSV: Now Zelophehad son of Hepher son of Gilead son of Machir son of Manasseh had no sons, but only daughters; and these are the names of his daughters: Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah.
text.Rotherham: But, Zelophehad, son of Hepher, son of Gilead, son of Machir, son of Manasseh, had no sons, but only daughters,—and, these, are the names of his daughters, Mahlah and Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah.

text.NRSV: They came before the priest Eleazar and Joshua son of Nun and the leaders, and said, “The Lord commanded Moses to give us an inheritance along with our male kin.” So according to the commandment of the Lord he gave them an inheritance among the kinsmen of their father.
text.Rotherham: And they had come near, before Eleazar the priest, and before Joshua son of Nun, and before the princes, saying, Yahweh, commanded Moses, to give us an inheritance in the midst of our brethren,—And he had given them, according to the bidding of Yahweh, an inheritance, in the midst of the brethren of their father.

text.NRSV: Thus there fell to Manasseh ten portions, besides the land of Gilead and Bashan, which is on the other side of the Jordan,
text.Rotherham: Thus there fell ten portions to Manasseh,—besides the land of Gilead, and Bashan, which is beyond the Jordan;

text.NRSV: because the daughters of Manasseh received an inheritance along with his sons. The land of Gilead was allotted to the rest of the Manassites.
text.Rotherham: because, the daughters of Manasseh, received an inheritance in the midst of his sons,—and, the land of Gilead, became the possession of the sons of Manasseh that remained.

text.NRSV: The territory of Manasseh reached from Asher to Michmethath, which is east of Shechem; then the boundary goes along southward to the inhabitants of En-tappuah.
text.Rotherham: So then the boundary of Manasseh was from Asher, Michmethath, which faceth Shechem,—and the boundary goeth along unto the right hand, unto the inhabitants of En-tappuah.

text.NRSV: The land of Tappuah belonged to Manasseh, but the town of Tappuah on the boundary of Manasseh belonged to the Ephraimites.
text.Rotherham: Manasseh, had the land of Tappuah,—but, Tappuah itself, towards the boundary of Manasseh, pertained unto the sons of Ephraim;

text.NRSV: Then the boundary went down to the Wadi Kanah. The towns here, to the south of the wadi, among the towns of Manasseh, belong to Ephraim. Then the boundary of Manasseh goes along the north side of the wadi and ends at the sea.
text.Rotherham: and the boundary goeth down to the ravine of Kanah, southward of the ravine, these cities, belong to Ephraim, in the midst of the cities of Manasseh,—but, the boundary of Manasseh, was on the north side of the ravine, and the extensions thereof were to the sea;

text.NRSV: The land to the south is Ephraim’s and that to the north is Manasseh’s, with the sea forming its boundary; on the north Asher is reached, and on the east Issachar.
text.Rotherham: southward, pertaineth to Ephraim, and, northward, to Manasseh, and the sea was the boundary thereof,—and they touch, Asher, on the north, and Issachar on the east.

text.NRSV: Within Issachar and Asher, Manasseh had Beth-shean and its villages, Ibleam and its villages, the inhabitants of Dor and its villages, the inhabitants of En-dor and its villages, the inhabitants of Taanach and its villages, and the inhabitants of Megiddo and its villages (the third is Naphath).
text.Rotherham: And Manasseh had—in Issachar and in Asher—Bethshean and her towns, and Ibleam and her towns, and the inhabitants of Dor and her towns, and the inhabitants of En-dor and her towns, and the inhabitants of Taanach and her towns, and the inhabitants of Megiddo, and her towns—the three heights,

text.NRSV: Yet the Manassites could not take possession of those towns; but the Canaanites continued to live in that land.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Manasseh, however, could not dispossess these cities,—but the Canaanites were determined to remain in this land;

text.NRSV: But when the Israelites grew strong, they put the Canaanites to forced labor, but did not utterly drive them out.
text.Rotherham: but, when the sons of Israel had waxed strong, they put the Canaanites under tribute,—though they, dispossessed, them not.

text.NRSV: The tribe of Joseph spoke to Joshua, saying, “Why have you given me but one lot and one portion as an inheritance, since we are a numerous people, whom all along the Lord has blessed?”
text.Rotherham: Then spake the sons of Joseph unto Joshua, saying,—Why hast thou given me, as an inheritance, but one lot and one portion, seeing that, I, am a numerous people, because hitherto hath Yahweh blessed me.

text.NRSV: And Joshua said to them, “If you are a numerous people, go up to the forest, and clear ground there for yourselves in the land of the Perizzites and the Rephaim, since the hill country of Ephraim is too narrow for you.”
text.Rotherham: And Joshua said unto them: If, a numerous people, thou art, get thee up to the forest, and cut down for thyself there, in the land of the Perizzites and of the Rephaim,—seeing that, too narrow for thee, is the hill country of Ephraim.

text.NRSV: The tribe of Joseph said, “The hill country is not enough for us; yet all the Canaanites who live in the plain have chariots of iron, both those in Beth-shean and its villages and those in the Valley of Jezreel.”
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Joseph said, The hill country is not enough for us,—and there are, chariots of iron, among all the Canaanites that dwell in the land of the valley, belonging both to them in Bethshean, and her towns, and to them in the valley of Jezreel.

text.NRSV: Then Joshua said to the house of Joseph, to Ephraim and Manasseh, “You are indeed a numerous people, and have great power; you shall not have one lot only,
text.Rotherham: And Joshua made answer unto the house of Joseph, to Ephraim and to Manasseh, saying,—A numerous people, thou art, and, great vigour, thou hast, thou shalt not have one lot only;

text.NRSV: but the hill country shall be yours, for though it is a forest, you shall clear it and possess it to its farthest borders; for you shall drive out the Canaanites, though they have chariots of iron, and though they are strong.”
text.Rotherham: for, the hill country, shall be thine, in that, a forest, it is, therefore canst thou cut it down, and thine shall be the extensions thereof,—for thou shalt dispossess the Canaanites, though, chariots of iron, they have, and though, strong, they are.

text.NRSV: Then the whole congregation of the Israelites assembled at Shiloh, and set up the tent of meeting there. The land lay subdued before them.
text.Rotherham: Then were gathered together all the assembly of the sons of Israel at Shiloh, and they set up there, the tent of meeting,—the land, having been subdued before them.

text.NRSV: There remained among the Israelites seven tribes whose inheritance had not yet been apportioned.
text.Rotherham: But there remained among the sons of Israel, to whom had not been apportioned their inheritance,—seven tribes.

text.NRSV: So Joshua said to the Israelites, “How long will you be slack about going in and taking possession of the land that the Lord, the God of your ancestors, has given you?
text.Rotherham: So Joshua said unto the sons of Israel,—How long, will ye be too slothful, to enter in and take possession of the land, which Yahweh God of your fathers, hath given unto you?

text.NRSV: Provide three men from each tribe, and I will send them out that they may begin to go throughout the land, writing a description of it with a view to their inheritances. Then come back to me.
text.Rotherham: Set forth for you three men for each tribe,—that I may send them, that they may arise—and go up and down in the land—and map it out as required for their inheritance, and come in unto me.

text.NRSV: They shall divide it into seven portions, Judah continuing in its territory on the south, and the house of Joseph in their territory on the north.
text.Rotherham: So shall they apportion it for themselves into seven parts,—Let, Judah, stay upon his boundary, on the south, and, the house of Joseph, stay upon their boundary, on the north,

text.NRSV: You shall describe the land in seven divisions and bring the description here to me; and I will cast lots for you here before the Lord our God.
text.Rotherham: but, ye, shall map out the [rest of the] land, into seven parts,—and bring in [your descriptions] unto me, here,—then will I cast lots for you here, before Yahweh our God.

text.NRSV: The Levites have no portion among you, for the priesthood of the Lord is their heritage; and Gad and Reuben and the half-tribe of Manasseh have received their inheritance beyond the Jordan eastward, which Moses the servant of the Lord gave them.”
text.Rotherham: For Levi hath no portion in your midst, for, the priesthood of Yahweh, is his inheritance,—and, Gad, and Reuben, and the half tribe of Manasseh, have received their inheritance, beyond the Jordan on the east, which Moses the servant of Yahweh gave unto them.

text.NRSV: So the men started on their way; and Joshua charged those who went to write the description of the land, saying, “Go throughout the land and write a description of it, and come back to me; and I will cast lots for you here before the Lord in Shiloh.”
text.Rotherham: So the men arose, and went,—and Joshua commanded them who were going, to map out the land, saying—Take your journey and go up and down in the land, and map it out, and return unto me, and, here, will I cast lots for you before Yahweh, in Shiloh.

text.NRSV: So the men went and traversed the land and set down in a book a description of it by towns in seven divisions; then they came back to Joshua in the camp at Shiloh,
text.Rotherham: So the men went, and passed through the land, and mapped it out by cities, into seven parts, upon a scroll,—and came in unto Joshua, unto the camp, in Shiloh.

text.NRSV: and Joshua cast lots for them in Shiloh before the Lord; and there Joshua apportioned the land to the Israelites, to each a portion.
text.Rotherham: And Joshua cast for them lots, in Shiloh, before Yahweh,—and Joshua there apportioned the land unto the sons of Israel, according to their portions.

text.NRSV: The lot of the tribe of Benjamin according to its families came up, and the territory allotted to it fell between the tribe of Judah and the tribe of Joseph.
text.Rotherham: Then came up the lot of the tribe of the sons of Benjamin, by their families,—and the boundary of their lot came out, between the sons of Judah and the sons of Joseph.

text.NRSV: On the north side their boundary began at the Jordan; then the boundary goes up to the slope of Jericho on the north, then up through the hill country westward; and it ends at the wilderness of Beth-aven.
text.Rotherham: And so their boundary, on the north border, was from the Jordan,—and the boundary goeth up unto the side of Jericho on the north, and goeth up in the hill country, westward, and the extensions thereof are towards the wilderness of Beth-aven;

text.NRSV: From there the boundary passes along southward in the direction of Luz, to the slope of Luz (that is, Bethel), then the boundary goes down to Ataroth-addar, on the mountain that lies south of Lower Beth-horon.
text.Rotherham: and the boundary passeth over from thence towards Luz, to the side of Luz, southwards, the same, is Bethel,—and the boundary goeth down to Ataroth-addah, by the mountain that is on the south of Beth-horon the nether;

text.NRSV: Then the boundary goes in another direction, turning on the western side southward from the mountain that lies to the south, opposite Beth-horon, and it ends at Kiriath-baal (that is, Kiriath-jearim), a town belonging to the tribe of Judah. This forms the western side.
text.Rotherham: then turneth the boundary and goeth round the west border, southward, from the mountain which faceth Beth-horon, southward, and so the extensions thereof are unto Kiriath-baal—the same, is Kiriath-jearim, a city of the sons of Judah,—this, is the west border.

text.NRSV: The southern side begins at the outskirts of Kiriath-jearim; and the boundary goes from there to Ephron, to the spring of the Waters of Nephtoah;
text.Rotherham: And, the border southward, is from the uttermost part of Kiriath-jearim,—and the boundary goeth out westward, yea it goeth out unto the fountain of the waters of Nephtoah;

text.NRSV: then the boundary goes down to the border of the mountain that overlooks the valley of the son of Hinnom, which is at the north end of the valley of Rephaim; and it then goes down the valley of Hinnom, south of the slope of the Jebusites, and downward to En-rogel;
text.Rotherham: and the boundary goeth down to the uttermost part of the mountain which faceth the valley of the son of Hinnom, which is in the Vale of Giants, northward,—and descendeth the valley of Hinnom, unto the side of the Jebusite, southward, and then descendeth to En-rogel;

text.NRSV: then it bends in a northerly direction going on to En-shemesh, and from there goes to Geliloth, which is opposite the ascent of Adummim; then it goes down to the Stone of Bohan, Reuben’s son;
text.Rotherham: and it turneth on the north, and goeth out at En-shemesh, and goeth out unto Geliloth, which is over against the ascent of Adummim,—and goeth down by the Stone of Bohan, son of Reuben;

text.NRSV: and passing on to the north of the slope of Beth-arabah it goes down to the Arabah;
text.Rotherham: and passeth along unto the side over against the Arabah, northward,—and goeth down toward the Arabah;

text.NRSV: then the boundary passes on to the north of the slope of Beth-hoglah; and the boundary ends at the northern bay of the Dead Sea, at the south end of the Jordan: this is the southern border.
text.Rotherham: and the boundary passeth along unto the side of Beth-hoglah, northward, and so the extensions of the boundary are unto the bay of the Salt Sea, northward, unto the end of the Jordan, southward,—This, is the south boundary.

text.NRSV: The Jordan forms its boundary on the eastern side. This is the inheritance of the tribe of Benjamin, according to its families, boundary by boundary all around.
text.Rotherham: And, the Jordan itself, boundeth it as an eastward border. This, is the inheritance of the sons of Benjamin, by the boundaries thereof round about, by their families.

text.NRSV: Now the towns of the tribe of Benjamin according to their families were Jericho, Beth-hoglah, Emek-keziz,
text.Rotherham: So then the cities of the tribe of the sons of Benjamin, by their families, are,—Jericho and Beth-hoglah, and Emekkeziz,

text.NRSV: Beth-arabah, Zemaraim, Bethel,
text.Rotherham: and Beth-arabah and Zemaraim, and Bethel,

text.NRSV: Avvim, Parah, Ophrah,
text.Rotherham: and Avvim and Parah and Ophrah,

text.NRSV: Chephar-ammoni, Ophni, and Geba—twelve towns with their villages:
text.Rotherham: and Chephar-ammoni and Ophni, and Geba,—twelve cities, with their villages:

text.NRSV: Gibeon, Ramah, Beeroth,
text.Rotherham: Gibeon and Ramah, and Beeroth,

text.NRSV: Mizpeh, Chephirah, Mozah,
text.Rotherham: and Mizpeh and Chephirah, and Mozah,

text.NRSV: Rekem, Irpeel, Taralah,
text.Rotherham: and Rekem and Irpeel, and Taralah,

text.NRSV: Zela, Haeleph, Jebus (that is, Jerusalem), Gibeah and Kiriath-jearim—fourteen towns with their villages. This is the inheritance of the tribe of Benjamin according to its families.
text.Rotherham: and Zelah, Eleph, and the Jebusite, the same, is Jerusalem, Gibeath [and] Kiriath, fourteen cities, with their villages. This, is the inheritance of the song of Benjamin, by their families.

text.NRSV: The second lot came out for Simeon, for the tribe of Simeon, according to its families; its inheritance lay within the inheritance of the tribe of Judah.
text.Rotherham: And the second lot came out, for Simeon, for the tribe of the sons of Simeon, by their families,—and their inheritance was in the midst of the inheritance of the sons of Judah.

text.NRSV: It had for its inheritance Beer-sheba, Sheba, Moladah,
text.Rotherham: And they had for their inheritance,—Beer-sheba or Sheba, and Moladah,

text.NRSV: Hazar-shual, Balah, Ezem,
text.Rotherham: and Hazar-shual, and Balah, and Ezem,

text.NRSV: Eltolad, Bethul, Hormah,
text.Rotherham: and Eltolad and Bethul, and Hormah,

text.NRSV: Ziklag, Beth-marcaboth, Hazar-susah,
text.Rotherham: and Ziklag and Beth-marcaboth, and Hazar-susah,

text.NRSV: Beth-lebaoth, and Sharuhen—thirteen towns with their villages;
text.Rotherham: and Beth-lebaoth, and Sharuhen,—thirteen cities, with their villages:

text.NRSV: Ain, Rimmon, Ether, and Ashan—four towns with their villages;
text.Rotherham: Ain, Rimmon, and Ether and Ashan,—four cities, with their villages,

text.NRSV: together with all the villages all around these towns as far as Baalath-beer, Ramah of the Negeb. This was the inheritance of the tribe of Simeon according to its families.
text.Rotherham: and all the villages that were round about these cities, as far as Baalath-beer, Ramah of the South. This, is the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Simeon, by their families:

text.NRSV: The inheritance of the tribe of Simeon formed part of the territory of Judah; because the portion of the tribe of Judah was too large for them, the tribe of Simeon obtained an inheritance within their inheritance.
text.Rotherham: Out of the portion of the sons of Judah, is the inheritance of the sons of Simeon,—for it came to pass that what was allotted to the sons of Judah, was too much for them, therefore did the sons of Simeon receive an inheritance in the midst of their inheritance.

text.NRSV: The third lot came up for the tribe of Zebulun, according to its families. The boundary of its inheritance reached as far as Sarid;
text.Rotherham: Then came up the third lot, for the sons of Zebulun, by their families,—and the boundary of their inheritance was as far as Sarid.

text.NRSV: then its boundary goes up westward, and on to Maralah, and touches Dabbesheth, then the wadi that is east of Jokneam;
text.Rotherham: And their boundary goeth up westward, even towards Maralah, and toucheth Dabbesheth,—and reacheth unto the ravine that faceth Jokneam;

text.NRSV: from Sarid it goes in the other direction eastward toward the sunrise to the boundary of Chisloth-tabor; from there it goes to Daberath, then up to Japhia;
text.Rotherham: and turneth back from Sarid, eastward, toward the rising of the sun, upon the boundary of Chisloth-tabor,—and goeth out unto Daberath, and ascendeth Japhia;

text.NRSV: from there it passes along on the east toward the sunrise to Gath-hepher, to Eth-kazin, and going on to Rimmon it bends toward Neah;
text.Rotherham: and, from thence, it passed along in front on the east, towards Gath-hepher, towards Eth-kazin,—and goeth out at Rimmon which turneth about towards Neah;

text.NRSV: then on the north the boundary makes a turn to Hannathon, and it ends at the valley of Iphtah-el;
text.Rotherham: and the boundary goeth round it, on the north to Hannathon,—and so the extensions thereof are the valley of Iphtah-el;

text.NRSV: and Kattath, Nahalal, Shimron, Idalah, and Bethlehem—twelve towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: and Kattath and Nahalal, and Shimron, and Idalah and Beth-lehem,—twelve cities, with their villages.

text.NRSV: This is the inheritance of the tribe of Zebulun, according to its families—these towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: This, is the inheritance of the sons of Zebulun, by their families,—these cities, with their villages.

text.NRSV: The fourth lot came out for Issachar, for the tribe of Issachar, according to its families.
text.Rotherham: For Issachar, came out the fourth lot,—for the sons of Issachar, by their families.

text.NRSV: Its territory included Jezreel, Chesulloth, Shunem,
text.Rotherham: And their boundary was,—Jezreel and Chesulloth, and Shunem,

text.NRSV: Hapharaim, Shion, Anaharath,
text.Rotherham: and Hapharaim and Shion, and Anaharath,

text.NRSV: Rabbith, Kishion, Ebez,
text.Rotherham: and Rabbith and Kishion, and Ebez,

text.NRSV: Remeth, En-gannim, En-haddah, Beth-pazzez;
text.Rotherham: and Remeth, and En-gannim, and Enhaddah, and Beth-pazzez;

text.NRSV: the boundary also touches Tabor, Shahazumah, and Beth-shemesh, and its boundary ends at the Jordan—sixteen towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: and the boundary toucheth Tabor and Shahazumah, and Beth-shemesh, and so the extensions of their boundary are to the Jordan,—sixteen cities, with their villages.

text.NRSV: This is the inheritance of the tribe of Issachar, according to its families—the towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: This, is the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Issachar, by their families,—the cities, with their villages.

text.NRSV: The fifth lot came out for the tribe of Asher according to its families.
text.Rotherham: Then came out the fifth lot, for the tribe of the sons of Asher, by their families.

text.NRSV: Its boundary included Helkath, Hali, Beten, Achshaph,
text.Rotherham: And their boundary was,—Helkath and Hali, and Beten and Achshaph,

text.NRSV: Allammelech, Amad, and Mishal; on the west it touches Carmel and Shihor-libnath,
text.Rotherham: and Allam-melech and Amad, and Mishal,—and it toucheth Carmel to the west, and Shihor-libnath;
